text: reading a new poem at open mic tonight, the one about dad's truck. dex is covering the espresso machine so i don't spill on myself mid-stanza
oh no the truck one?? the one that made you cry in the car
that's the one. figure if it wrecks me it might as well wreck a room full of strangers first
solid strategy tbh. what time do you go on
9ish, after some guy who does spoken word about crypto. very humbling lineup
i'll call you after. proud of you, weirdo
Hey did you actually get up and read something at that open mic thing last night? Reese said you did
I did. Three minutes on Nia's cat destroying my zine drafts. Crowd loved it more than my poetry
Ha, that tracks. Proud of you kid
Thanks pop. Joon's already bugging me to turn it into a real zine piece for the bookshop run
Good, send me one when you print em. Framing it above the workbench
Deal, don't let Mom see the cat line though, she'll think it's about her
hey did Reese tell you she's coming up for the open mic thing at Alchemy thursday
she mentioned it! said you're finally reading the piece about grandpa's boat
reading it out loud in front of Dex is gonna be a whole ordeal but yes
i can already tell i'm going to cry into my complimentary cortado
there is no complimentary cortado mom you'll pay full price like everyone else
worth it. put me down for a seat near the back
who's got a slot at open mic thursday, sign-up sheet fills fast
just printed the new Thread & Bone batch, drop me a DM if your shop wants copies
reading recc: Ross Gay's Catalog of Unabashed Gratitude, still my favorite
anyone doing the Providence Poetry Festival volunteer shift this weekend?
the alchemy oat-milk latte is criminally good today, come through before my shift ends
zine layout night at mine friday, byo scissors and a poem
hot take: the best line breaks happen on the bus to warwick
selling a few back issues at Symposium Books this week, tell your friends
who else is submitting to Rust & Marrow before the 31st
phoebe bridgers on repeat while i revise, send help
